stories.headline: Using AI Wisely: A Pastor’s Encouragement to His Church
New Study Explores Omnicidal Futures Involving AI
stories.summary: Researchers have published a taxonomy of potential omnicidal futures involving artificial intelligence, sparking discussion about the risks and consequences of advanced AI. The study aims to contribute to a deeper understanding of the potential dangers of AI and how they can be mitigated.

stories.headline: Argument Against Open-Source AI Deemed Flawed
stories.summary: A recent article argues that the arguments against open-source AI are poorly founded and lack substance. The author presents a case for why open-source AI can be beneficial and why the concerns raised against it are not convincing.

stories.headline: New Resource Available for Learning WebGPU with C++
stories.summary: A new tutorial resource has been made available for developers looking to learn WebGPU using C++. The resource provides a comprehensive guide to getting started with WebGPU and exploring its capabilities.

stories.headline: Extensive Tutorial Resource for Modern OpenGL Released
stories.summary: A detailed tutorial resource for learning Modern OpenGL has been released, offering developers a comprehensive guide to the technology. The resource covers a wide range of topics, from the basics to advanced techniques, making it a valuable tool for both beginners and experienced developers.

stories.headline: The Greatest Is Love
US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners, citing forced labour concerns
Uncertainty Surrounds Fate of Trafficked Indonesian Babies
stories.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but their future remains uncertain. The incident has raised concerns about human trafficking and the need for greater cooperation between countries to protect vulnerable individuals.

stories.headline: Trump Links Saudi Nuclear Deal to Israel Recognition
stories.summary: The US president has made it clear that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia is contingent on the country recognizing Israel. The move is seen as a significant development in the Middle East peace process and could have far-reaching implications for the region.

stories.headline: The first known runaway AI agent - or a very bad marketing stunt?
Visual 6502 Brings Classic Chip to Life in Browser
stories.summary: An interactive JavaScript simulator visualizes every transistor of the iconic 6502 CPU, letting users step through code and watch signals in real time. It serves both as an educational tool and a nostalgic showcase for retro computing fans.

stories.headline: Echo Cuts AI Costs by Two‑Thirds Using Open‑Weight Model Ensemble
stories.summary: Echo stitches together several open‑source language models, such as GLM‑5.2 and Kimi‑K2.7, to handle a range of tasks instead of relying on a single heavyweight model. The ensemble delivers near‑Fable quality output while slashing compute expenses to about a third of traditional approaches.

stories.headline: New Solid‑State Atomic Channel Boosts Rare‑Earth Purification
stories.summary: Researchers at the University of Chicago unveiled a chip‑scale device that uses atomic‑scale channels to selectively filter rare‑earth ions, offering a cleaner, energy‑efficient alternative to chemical leaching. This technology could streamline supply chains for critical magnets and electronics.

stories.headline: Claude‑Thermos Auto‑Keeps Claude Sessions Alive
stories.summary: Claude‑Thermos is an open‑source wrapper that periodically pings the Claude API to prevent session timeouts, sparing users from re‑prompting. It streamlines workflows for anyone who relies on Claude for continuous interactions.

stories.headline: Screenpipe Turns Your Desktop into an AI‑Readable Memory Log
stories.summary: Screenpipe records local video and audio streams, indexing them for instant retrieval by AI agents, which enables automation of repetitive tasks and the creation of SOPs. Its privacy‑first design ensures all data stays on the user’s machine.

stories.headline: OneCLI Secures AI Agents with On‑Demand Credential Vault
stories.summary: OneCLI provides an open‑source gateway that supplies secrets to AI agents only when required, eliminating permanent storage of credentials in prompts. It integrates with existing vaults and enforces zero‑trust access for AI‑driven workflows.

stories.headline: Founders Warn U.S. Against Cutting Off Chinese Open‑Weight AI
stories.summary: A coalition of startup leaders petitioned the U.S. government, arguing that restricting Chinese open‑source models would hamper innovation and global competition. They advocate for collaborative AI development rather than isolationist policies.

stories.headline: Palmier Pro Launches as Free macOS Video Editor Powered by AI
stories.summary: Palmier Pro offers a native macOS editing suite with built‑in generative AI tools for transitions, effects, and content creation, all running locally via an MCP server. The project aims to democratize high‑quality video production without cloud fees.

stories.headline: Trump’s Saudi Nuclear Pact Ditches Traditional U.S. Safeguards
stories.summary: The agreement lets Saudi Arabia pursue nuclear energy without the usual U.S. non‑proliferation conditions, drawing criticism from Washington and Israel. Analysts warn the move could destabilize regional security dynamics.

stories.headline: BBC Returns to Iranian School Site Six Months After Deadly Strike
stories.summary: A teacher and a mother recount the lingering trauma of the Minab school attack that killed 120 children. The report highlights ongoing grief and calls for accountability for the tragedy.

stories.headline: US Justice Department Drops Subpoenas Against New York Times
stories.summary: The US Justice Department has withdrawn its subpoenas against the New York Times, following a federal judge's sharp questioning. The move marks a significant development in the ongoing tension between the government and the press.

stories.headline: Justice Dept. drops 'New York Times' subpoenas under pressure from judge
Trump to impose double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries
Flighty New Connection Assistant Feature
France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated
Building on ATProto
For a 2nd time, House approves resolution to end the war in Iran in a rebuke to Trump
'Let's enjoy the ride' says Elon Musk, as AI fears mount
Ex-governor convicted of murdering a pregnant student in Kenya
Software Factories Falter: Engineering Alone Won’t Cut It
stories.summary: The piece argues that building software at factory scale often collapses because it ignores organizational and human factors. Success requires a broader engineering context beyond just tooling.

stories.headline: Google and Tesla Shares Dive as AI Spending Spooks Investors
stories.summary: Heavy investment in AI is rattling the market, sending both companies' stocks sharply lower. Analysts warn that the financial payoff from AI remains uncertain.

stories.headline: Houthi Threats to Saudi Shipping Heighten Middle East Conflict Fears
stories.summary: Yemen’s Iran‑backed Houthi rebels have warned they may target vessels in a key Saudi shipping lane. The escalation could widen regional hostilities and strain global trade.

stories.headline: UK Band Refines Post‑Punk Edge with Uplifting New Album
stories.summary: The group smooths its traditionally jagged sound into a more melodic, hopeful record. Listeners hear echoes of classic British rock throughout the release.

stories.headline: Interactive Map Charts Every James Bond Filming Location
stories.summary: Daring Fireball has compiled a global map pinpointing sites used in Bond movies. The resource aims to delight both casual fans and dedicated enthusiasts.

stories.headline: Police Eject Leading Scientists from ADA Conference, Researchers React
stories.summary: Law enforcement removed several prominent researchers from a meeting of the American Diabetes Association. The scientific community has publicly responded, questioning the justification.

stories.headline: John Dvorak’s 1987 Take on IBM’s Cable‑Free PS/2 Still Holds Up
stories.summary: In a vintage Computer Chronicles interview, Dvorak praised the PS/2’s innovative design that eliminated internal cables. His technical insight proved more accurate than many of his other predictions.

stories.headline: Short Wave Explores Avocado Evolution, Birdsong, and Egyptian Warrior Queens
stories.summary: NPR’s podcast delves into the genetic roots of avocados, the building blocks of birdsong, and the legacy of ancient Egyptian female warriors. Each segment blends science with surprising historical narratives.

stories.headline: 98.css Brings Retro Windows 98 UI to Modern Web Pages
stories.summary: The CSS framework recreates the look and feel of Windows 98, complete with a functional status bar. Its nostalgic charm has earned it strong traction among developers.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Cuts Over 100 Federal Advisory Boards, NASA’s Oversight at Risk
stories.summary: The White House has terminated or suspended more than a hundred advisory committees, reducing external input for agencies. Critics warn that bodies like NASA will face diminished accountability to the public.

stories.headline: Indian activist ends 26‑day hunger strike to prevent violence
stories.summary: Sonam Wangchuk, backing youth protesters demanding education reform, called off his fast after 26 days, saying he wanted to stop any potential clashes. He urged dialogue and warned that continued protest could turn violent.

stories.headline: Trump‑appointed council moves to roll back historic preservation rules
stories.summary: A federal review board filled with Trump allies is set to rewrite regulations that protect historic sites, a change critics say could speed up the president’s D.C. memorial project. The shift threatens protections for landmarks nationwide.

stories.headline: Chinese mathematician becomes first woman to win Fields Medal
stories.summary: In a historic first, a Chinese researcher earned the Fields Medal, the highest honor in mathematics, amid concerns about a brain drain to the United States. Her win coincides with a wave of Chinese scholars returning home despite a hostile U.S. academic climate.

stories.headline: Nicaragua’s parliament considers barring opposition from upcoming elections
stories.summary: Lawmakers debated a bill that would exclude rival parties from the ballot, following President Daniel Ortega’s call for a “wall and blockade” against dissent. The proposal signals a further tightening of authoritarian control ahead of the vote.

stories.headline: Chinese animal‑rights activists shift fight online after censorship
stories.summary: After a brutal killing of a dog and her puppies in Guangdong sparked outrage, activists were blocked from posting domestically and moved their campaign to overseas platforms. The incident highlights growing online repression of animal‑welfare advocacy in China.

stories.headline: Ukrainian drones strike Wildberries warehouses, rattling Russia’s e‑commerce giant
stories.summary: A series of drone attacks hit several distribution centers of Wildberries, Russia’s largest online retailer, within days. The hits illustrate how Ukraine’s strike campaign is extending into Russia’s commercial infrastructure.

stories.headline: CJP protests erupt into India’s largest student movement in years
stories.summary: What began as a leak of exam papers sparked the CJP (Common Justice Protest) rally, quickly expanding into a nationwide student uprising against the Modi government. Demonstrators cite education reforms and broader democratic concerns as driving forces.

stories.headline: Pop star’s new album swaps synth‑pop for gritty, self‑referential rock
stories.summary: On the follow‑up to “BRAT,” the artist abandons polished pop for a deliberately rough‑around‑edges rock sound that riffs on fame and industry clichés. Critics note the record feels like a backstage glimpse at celebrity life.

stories.headline: Brent crude breaches $100 a barrel as Middle East conflict intensifies
stories.summary: Oil prices jumped over 6% on Thursday, pushing Brent crude above the $100 mark for the first time since May. The surge reflects heightened geopolitical risk from the escalating war in the region.

stories.headline: Teachers use blockbuster‑style Odyssey adaptation to revive ancient epic
stories.summary: A new cinematic version of Homer’s “The Odyssey” is being rolled out in classrooms to make the classic more engaging for students. Educators hope the high‑budget feel will spark interest in ancient literature.
